contexts.context: ...inextricably self-involved; a nightmare sensation of <mark>impotence</mark> and vain endeavour weighs upon me, whether my own...
...——O I am sick of this most lame and <mark>impotent</mark> hero?
...Horatio. But there is the same lame and <mark>impotent</mark> conclusion in every thing he does. Soon after this,...
...the marriage annulled on the grounds of her husband's <mark>impotency</mark> in 1476. Back
